message:
test(codec/conformance): split non-canonical leb128 test into valid-accept + impossible-reject

Paired update to the conformance suite for the codec decode fix in the
prior commit. Before: the single test
conformance_non_canonical_leb128_zero_is_accepted double-declared `wire`
(twice!) and then only exercised the second declaration, which was actually
an IMPOSSIBLE frame (tagged_len=0 but serial+ident consume 4 header bytes).
The test name said "accepted" but the assertion was expect_err — the name
and the spec drift in the doc comment both lied about what was being pinned.

The codec fix in the previous commit changed decode_raw to derive data_len
from observed leb128 byte count (serial_len + ident_len), not from the
canonical encoded_length() helper. That makes the "valid non-canonical"
case — where tagged_len correctly counts the bytes ACTUALLY on the wire —
decodeable for the first time. The "impossible non-canonical" case, where
tagged_len disagrees with the header bytes consumed, still has to fail.

Split into two tests that each pin exactly one contract:

1. conformance_valid_non_canonical_leb128_headers_are_accepted
   wire = frame_verbatim([0x84, 0x00], [0x81, 0x00], [0xE3, 0x00], [])
   tagged_len=4 over 2 bytes, serial=1 over 2 bytes, ident=99 over 2 bytes.
   serial_len + ident_len = 4 matches tagged_len, so data_len = 0 and the
   frame decodes cleanly to DecodedPdu { serial: 1, pdu: Pdu::Invalid {
   ident: 99 } }. Pins the new forgiving-decode behavior.

2. conformance_impossible_non_canonical_header_fails_sanity_check
   wire = frame_verbatim([0x80, 0x00], [0x81, 0x00], [0xE3, 0x00], [])
   tagged_len=0 over 2 bytes but serial + ident consume 4 bytes, so the
   overflow arithmetic underflows. Must fail with "sizes don't make sense".
   Pins the rejection-on-impossible-arithmetic contract.

Extract a small frame_verbatim(tagged_len, serial, ident, data) helper next
to the existing frame() builder. Needed specifically because these tests
want to assemble non-canonical leb128 bytes on the wire — frame() runs
inputs through a canonical encoder and would normalize exactly the cases
under test. Docstring explains the distinction.

Also fix the file header's wire-format note: replace
"tagged_len - encoded_length(serial) - encoded_length(ident) bytes" with
"tagged_len - encoded byte length of serial - encoded byte length of ident"
so it accurately reflects the new observe-don't-reconstruct semantics
without referencing a helper that no longer appears in the computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
